artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow student-facing academic-integrity claim; the source says may be regarded as plagiarism, so the claim avoids stronger mandatory wording.
Approved as a narrow AI-tool-treatment claim; the source says restrictions or prohibitions may apply depending on the class.
Approved as a narrow privacy risk claim tied to the university announcement's personal and confidential information warning.

artifacts.title: Kobe University staged generative AI source review
artifacts.summary: Kobe University's official student-facing announcement states that unchanged generative AI text in reports may be regarded as plagiarism, that some classes may restrict or prohibit generative AI use, and that entering confidential or personal information into generative AI may create leakage or disclosure risk. An official English version was also collected, while faculty-level materials were kept out of university-wide claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are based on Kobe University's 2023 student-facing announcement and should not be read as a full current AI governance framework.
Faculty-level report-writing materials were discovered but not used to make university-wide claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
